city council, Mr.. Robertson said:


"After the decision of the court
one would have thought that Mayor
Pratt would await the decision OF
the city council. Instead of that he
caused copy of the charges to be
filed with the board of police com.
missioners, which is now attempt.
ing to try them, without the testi
mony Of the plaintiff being heard
| have never seen an act SO fla.
grant and in such open violation OF
the law. There is no politics in
this thing. 1 don't care whether
Mayor Pratt is democrat or re-
publican, this matter should be In
vestigated on it's merits and the
truth heard. l appear here for j
H. Elliott, an honest and just old
man, defender of the Union, and
member of the G. A. R., who has
filed these charges. There are nc
gamblers, saloonmen or bad women
behind this case, and when it is
heard we want priests and preach
ers, and all other citizens, to be
present. We want only justice and
the fair name of the city of Spo
kane cleansed of the stain that has
been applied to it through the &C
tons set forth in these charges. Nc
subordinate board ought to have
the right to forestall justice in this
case"


The board of police commission
ers was ordered to make no find
ings in it's present inquiry unit
after the city council acted, and Il
was further ordered to see tina
John T. Sullivan was on hand wher
the official trial is called.


At the outset there was talk Of ar
early date for trial of the Sullivan
charges, and Councilman Lamber
introduced a resolution setting the
hearing for October 81. On account
of the absence from the city of the
sewer, water and light committed
about this time it was agreed to Sc
the date for a week later.
